rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Depositions Of Pltfs Gary Grote And Janice Grote Beyond The Distance Permitted Y Code Of Civil Procedure 2025.250
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 18, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs DAVID DRIVER, EMILIA RALLAPALLI's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Plaintiff's Gary Grote And Janice Grote Beyond The Distance Permitted Y Code Of Civil Procedure 2025.250 Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Pursuant to the ex parte Order issued June 19, 2014, this matter has been stayed for 60 days to permit Plaintiff GARY E. GROTE to obtain separate and independent counsel. The Motion to Compel the Depositions of Plaintiff GARY E. GROTE and JANICE GROTE is therefore CONTINUED to October 3, 2014 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301. Moving party to give notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ZACHARY LUKE VS. GEORGE VALVERDE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn; Req. For Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 18, 2014, Line 4: PETITIONER ZACHARY LUKE'S Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus. Denied. Petitioner has not established that the administrative decision is contrary to the weight of the evidence. The evidence supports the hearing officer's conclusion that the incorrect time of arrest was a clerical error and that Officer Woods' reports are trustworthy. (Administrative Record ("AR") CT000008-CT000009, DS-367; CT 000015-CT000017, Incident Report; CT000047-CT000048, Hearing transcript, pp. 10:22-11:4.) The evidence supports the hearing officer's conclusion that Officer Woods had reasonable cause to believe Petitioner was driving a motor vehicle while under the influence of alcohol, and therefore, probable cause to arrest Petitioner. Upon making contact with Petitioner, Officer Woods smelled alcohol and noticed Petitioner had watery, bloodshot eyes. (AR CT000008, DS-367; CT000015, Incident Report.) Petitioner was unable to properly perform the field sobriety tests and failed the Preliminary Alcohol Screening tests. (AR CT000016-CT000017, Incident Report.) The evidence supports the hearing officer's conclusion that Petitioner was properly admonished. In an administrative proceeding the determination of credibility is within the province of the hearing officer. (Mullin v. Dept. of Real Estate (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 295, 300-01.) Petitioner has not established that the hearing officer's subjective interpretation of the evidence was incorrect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RODOLFO CASTRO et al VS. WILLIAM C. LYNN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 18, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT WILLIAM C. LYNN, TRUSTEE OF THE WILLIAM C. LYNN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is stricken in its entirety pursuant to CCP § 436(b). The Court sustained Defendant's demurrer to the First Amended Complaint with leave to amend on February 13, 2014. Plaintiffs failed to amend the complaint within 10 days as required by CRC 3.1320(g). When Defendant moved to have the First Amended Complaint dismissed for failure to timely amend in April 2014, the Court issued an Order requiring Plaintiffs to file and serve the SAC by April 25, 2014. Plaintiffs failed to comply with this Order and did not serve the SAC until May 21, 2014. Plaintiffs fail to make a showing of good cause for why the Court's Order was not complied with. The evidence submitted in support of Plaintiffs' opposition is not properly authenticated and may not be considered. Even if the Court were to consider it, the evidence shows that Plaintiffs' counsel's car troubles did not begin until April 26, 2014, after the deadline to file and serve the SAC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ICHO VS. BALDWIN BOK YUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 18, 2014, Line 8: DEFENDANTS CHERIE LAU, BOAZ YUNG, and BALDWIN YUNG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is overruled and the Motion to Strike is denied. Ownership of stock is not a prerequisite for the application of the alter ego doctrine. (See Riddle v. Leuschner (1959) 51 Cal.2d 574 [finding defendant was the alter ego of one corporation in which she held no stock].) Rather, the analysis focuses on the question of control. (See, e.g., Sonora Diamond Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 523, 538 ["the courts will ignore the corporate entity and deem the corporation's acts to be those of the persons or organizations actually controlling the corporation, in most instances the equitable owners"].) Additionally, Defendants' contention that the First Amended Complaint lacks allegations of wrongdoing that could amount to the imposition of liability against Defendants Lau and Boaz Yung fails in light of dozens of such allegations contained in the pleading. (See, e.g., Dem. 11, 13, 14, 17, 18, 20(A) and (G), 21(A) and (G), 22(A) and (G).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
